comments.body: which is to say.... new OrRequestMatcher(Stream.of(new String[] {"/admin/", "*/admin/"}) .map(path -> PathPatternRequestMatcher.withDefaults().matcher(path)) .toArray(RequestMatcher[]::new)) To replace your antmatcher with a single (or) requestmatcher

answers.body:
There is no public contsructor in PathPatternRequestMatcher that takes a String argument so you need to do the following:

private RequestMatcher[] permittedAntMatchers() {
    return Stream.of(new String[] {"/admin/", "*/admin/"})
        .map(path -> PathPatternRequestMatcher.withDefaults().matcher(path))
        .toArray(RequestMatcher[]::new);
}

title: Migrate AntPathRequestMatcher to PathPatternRequestMatcher
body:
I have this code which I want to migrate:

private RequestMatcher[] permittedAntMatchers() {
    return Stream.concat(
                    "/admin/", "*/admin/"
            )
            .map(AntPathRequestMatcher::antMatcher)
            .toArray(RequestMatcher[]::new);
  }

I migrated it to this:

private RequestMatcher[] permittedAntMatchers() {
        return Stream.concat(
                        "/admin/", "*/admin/"
                )
                .map(PathPatternRequestMatcher::new)
                .toArray(RequestMatcher[]::new);
      }

But I get error:

'PathPatternRequestMatcher(org.springframework.web.util.pattern.PathPattern)' has private access in 'org.springframework.security.web.servlet.util.matcher.PathPatternRequestMatcher'

What is the proper way to migrate the code?
